words.definitionEn: having the colour of fresh snow or of milk
the colour of fresh snow or of milk
used in questions to ask about the name, identity or function of one or more people
having the colour of a clear sky or the sea on a clear day
used in questions to ask the reason for or purpose of something
the colour of a clear sky or the sea on a clear day
the woman that somebody is married to; a married woman
words.exampleEn: I met my wife at university.

words.definitionEn: used for talking about or predicting the future
words.exampleEn: You'll be in time if you hurry.

words.definitionEn: to be the most successful in a competition, race, battle, etc.
an opening in the wall or roof of a building, car, etc., usually covered with glass, that allows light and air to come in and people to see out; the glass in a window
words.exampleEn: out of the window, She looked out of the window.

words.definitionEn: an alcoholic drink made from the juice of grapes that has been left to ferment. There are many different kinds of wine.
the coldest season of the year, between autumn and spring
in the company or presence of somebody/something
not having, experiencing or showing something
words.exampleEn: They had gone two days without food.

words.definitionEn: an adult female human
very good, pleasant or a lot of fun
words.exampleEn: This is a wonderful opportunity to invest in new markets.

words.definitionEn: a single unit of language that means something and can be spoken or written
words.exampleEn: Do not write more than 200 words.

words.definitionEn: a vehicle (smaller than a ship) that travels on water, moved by oars, sails or a motor
the job that a person does especially in order to earn money
words.exampleEn: He started work as a security guard.

words.definitionEn: to do something that involves physical or mental effort, especially as part of a job
words.exampleEn: I can't work if I'm cold.

words.definitionEn: a person who works, especially one who does a particular kind of work
the earth, with all its countries, peoples and natural features
the whole physical structure of a human or an animal
words.exampleEn: The human body is a complex structure.

words.definitionEn: used in polite offers or invitations
to make letters or numbers on a surface, especially using a pen or a pencil
words.exampleEn: In some countries children don't start learning to read and write until they are six.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is writing books, stories, articles, etc.
the activity of writing, in contrast to reading, speaking, etc.
words.exampleEn: Our son’s having problems with his reading and writing (= at school)

words.definitionEn: not right or correct
words.exampleEn: I got all the answers wrong.

words.definitionEn: the period from 1 January to 31 December, that is 365 or 366 days, divided into 12 months
having the colour of lemons or butter
the colour of lemons or butter
used to answer a question and say that something is correct or true
on the day before today
words.exampleEn: A company spokeswoman said yesterday that no final decision had been made yet.

words.definitionEn: the day before today
used as the subject or object of a verb or after a preposition to refer to the person or people being spoken or written to
words.exampleEn: You said you knew the way.

words.definitionEn: having lived or existed for only a short time; not fully developed
words.exampleEn: Young babies need to be wrapped up warmly.

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to the person or people being spoken or written to
used when the person or people being spoken to both cause and are affected by an action
a set of printed pages that are fastened inside a cover so that you can turn them and read them
words.exampleEn: His desk was covered with piles of books.

words.definitionEn: a little more or less than; a little before or after
a strong shoe that covers the foot and ankle and often the lower part of the leg
feeling tired and impatient because you have lost interest in somebody/something or because you have nothing to do
words.exampleEn: There was a bored expression on her face.

words.definitionEn: not interesting; making you feel tired and impatient
to come out of your mother’s body at the beginning of your life
used with plural nouns to mean ‘the two’ or ‘the one as well as the other’
used with plural nouns to mean ‘the two’ or ‘the one as well as the other’
a glass or plastic container, usually round with straight sides and a narrow neck, used especially for storing liquids
a container made of wood, thick card, metal, etc. with a flat stiff base and sides and often a lid (= cover), used especially for holding solid things
words.exampleEn: in a box, Everything we owned was neatly packed in cardboard boxes.

words.definitionEn: a male child or a young male person
a man or boy that somebody has a romantic or sexual relationship with
from one side to the other side
words.exampleEn: It's too wide. We can't swim across.

words.definitionEn: a type of food made from flour, water and usually yeast mixed together and baked
a short period of time when you stop what you are doing and rest, eat, etc.
words.exampleEn: She was on her lunch break.

words.definitionEn: to be damaged and separated into two or more parts, as a result of force; to damage something in this way
words.exampleEn: All the windows broke with the force of the blast.

words.definitionEn: the first meal of the day
words.exampleEn: They were having breakfast when I arrived.

words.definitionEn: from one side to the other side of something
to come to a place with somebody/something
words.exampleEn: bring somebody/something (with you), Don't forget to bring your books with you.

words.definitionEn: a boy or man who has the same parents as another person
having the colour of earth or coffee
the colour of earth or coffee
words.exampleEn: leaves of various shades of brown

words.definitionEn: the process of doing something in order to make something happen or to deal with a situation
words.exampleEn: The time has come for action if these beautiful animals are to survive.

words.definitionEn: to make something, especially a building, by putting parts together
words.exampleEn: build (something), They have permission to build 200 new homes.

words.definitionEn: a structure such as a house or school that has a roof and walls
a large road vehicle that carries passengers, especially one that travels along a fixed route and stops regularly to let people get on and off
words.exampleEn: by bus, Shall we walk or go by bus?

words.definitionEn: the activity of making, buying, selling or supplying goods or services for money
words.exampleEn: It's been a pleasure to do business with you.

words.definitionEn: having a lot to do; perhaps not free to do something else because you are working on something
used to introduce a word or phrase that contrasts with what was said before
words.exampleEn: I got it wrong. It wasn't the red one but the blue one.

words.definitionEn: a soft yellow food made from cream, used in cooking and for spreading on bread
to obtain something by paying money for it
words.exampleEn: buy (something), Where did you buy that dress?

words.definitionEn: used for showing how or in what way something is done
words.exampleEn: The house is heated by gas.

words.definitionEn: goodbye
on the subject of somebody/something; in connection with somebody/something
a place where you can buy drinks and simple meals. Alcohol is not usually served in British or American cafes.
words.exampleEn: There are small shops and pavement cafes around every corner.

words.definitionEn: a sweet food made from a mixture of flour, eggs, butter, sugar, etc. that is baked in an oven. Cakes are made in various shapes and sizes and are often decorated, for example with cream or icing.
the act of speaking to somebody on the phone
words.exampleEn: to get/receive a call from somebody

words.definitionEn: to give somebody/something a particular name; to use a particular name or title when you are talking to somebody
words.exampleEn: call somebody/something + noun, They decided to call the baby Mark.

words.definitionEn: a piece of equipment for taking photographs, moving pictures or television pictures. It can be a separate item or part of another device.
words.exampleEn: Just point the camera and press the button.

words.definitionEn: used to say that it is possible for somebody/something to do something, or for something to happen
a thing that you do for interest or pleasure
the negative of can; can not
words.exampleEn: I cannot believe the price of the tickets!

words.definitionEn: a person who performs on the stage, on television or in films, especially as a profession
words.exampleEn: Both lead actors (= the ones who play the main parts) are outstanding.

words.definitionEn: having the form and size used at the beginning of a sentence or a name
words.exampleEn: English is written with a capital ‘E’.

words.definitionEn: the most important town or city of a country or region, where the government operates from
words.exampleEn: Cairo is the capital of Egypt.

words.definitionEn: a road vehicle with an engine and four wheels that can carry a small number of passengers
words.exampleEn: Paula got into the car and drove off.

words.definitionEn: a woman who performs on the stage, on television or in films, especially as a profession
words.exampleEn: The Oscar-winning actress has taken a break from acting.

words.definitionEn: thick, stiff paper; a piece of this for writing on
the series of jobs that a person has in a particular area of work, usually involving more responsibility as time passes
a long pointed orange root vegetable
to support the weight of somebody/something and take them or it from place to place; to take somebody/something from one place to another
words.exampleEn: carry somebody/something, He was carrying a large bag.

words.definitionEn: a small animal with soft fur that people often keep as a pet. Cats catch and kill birds and mice.
a small disc on which sound or information is recorded. CDs can be played or read on various types of machines, including CD players and computers. (the abbreviation for ‘compact disc’)
words.exampleEn: on CD, His albums are available on CD and online.

words.definitionEn: a coin and unit of money worth 1% of the main unit of money in many countries, for example of the US dollar or of the euro
words.exampleEn: A one-minute phone call to the UK cost 10 cents.

words.definitionEn: the middle point or part of something
words.exampleEn: He walked to the centre of the circle.

words.definitionEn: a period of 100 years
words.exampleEn: A century ago, Valparaiso was the country's main port.

words.definitionEn: a piece of furniture for one person to sit on, with a back, a seat and four legs
at or to a higher place
words.exampleEn: Put it on the shelf above.

words.definitionEn: the act or result of something becoming different
words.exampleEn: change in something, We're hoping for a change in the weather.

words.definitionEn: to become different
words.exampleEn: Rick hasn't changed. He looks exactly the same as he did at school.

words.definitionEn: to put something together with something else so as to increase the size, number, amount, etc.
words.exampleEn: add something, Next add the flour.

words.definitionEn: a diagram, lists of figures, etc. that shows information
words.exampleEn: Some shares have lost two-thirds of their value since being issued (see chart).

words.definitionEn: costing little money or less money than you expected
to examine something to see if it is correct, safe or acceptable
words.exampleEn: check something, She gave me the minutes of the meeting to read and check.

words.definitionEn: a type of food made from milk that can be either soft or hard and is usually white or yellow in colour; a particular type of this food
a large bird that is often kept for its eggs or meat
words.exampleEn: They keep chickens in the back yard.

words.definitionEn: a young human who is not yet an adult
a hard brown sweet food made from cocoa beans, used in cooking to add taste to cakes, etc. or eaten as a sweet
to decide which thing or person you want out of the ones that are available
a building in which films are shown
details of where somebody lives or works and where letters, etc. can be sent
a large and important town
words.exampleEn: Parking is difficult in the city centre.

words.definitionEn: a group of students who are taught together
words.exampleEn: in the/your class, We were in the same class at school.

words.definitionEn: a room where a class of children or students is taught
not dirty
to remove the dirt or dust from something
words.exampleEn: I spent all day cooking and cleaning.

words.definitionEn: to go up something towards the top
words.exampleEn: climb (up) something, to climb a mountain/tree

words.definitionEn: at or to a higher place or position than something/somebody
words.exampleEn: The water came above our knees.

words.definitionEn: an instrument for measuring and showing time, in a room, on the wall of a building or on a computer screen (not worn or carried like a watch)
to put something into a position so that it covers an opening; to get into this position
the things that you wear, such as trousers, dresses and jackets
words.exampleEn: I bought some new clothes for the trip.

words.definitionEn: an organization for people who share an interest or do a sport or activity together
a piece of outdoor clothing that is worn over other clothes to keep warm or dry. Coats have sleeves (= parts covering the arms) and may be long or short.
the roasted seeds (called coffee beans) of a tropical bush; a powder made from them
having a lower than usual temperature; having a temperature lower than the human body
words.exampleEn: I'm cold. Turn the heating up.

words.definitionEn: a common illness that affects the nose and/or throat, making you cough, sneeze, etc.
(in the UK) a place where students go to study or to receive training after they have left school
the appearance that things have that results from the way in which they reflect light. Red, orange and green are colours.
to move to or towards a person or place
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., He left and said he was never coming back.

words.definitionEn: happening often; existing in large numbers or in many places
a business organization that makes money by producing or selling goods or services
to examine people or things to see how they are similar and how they are different
words.exampleEn: compare A and B, It is interesting to compare their situation and ours.
